title: MCP Server Reconnect and Cleanup

activity_flows.name: reconnect(failed_only)
steps: NO  → await cleanup_all() [teardown all servers in reverse order]

activity_flows.name: _refresh_active_servers()
steps: YES → _active_servers = [s for s in _all_servers if s not in _failed_server_set]

activity_flows.name: cleanup_all()
steps: CATCH asyncio.CancelledError → if suppress_cancelled_error: log+record; else raise
CATCH Exception → log exception, record in errors

activity_flows.name: _cleanup_servers(servers)
activity_flows.note: Used during connect_all() error recovery (not full teardown)
steps: CATCH asyncio.CancelledError → suppress or re-raise per flag
CATCH Exception → log + record in errors

activity_flows.name: _cleanup_server(server)
steps: YES AND worker.is_done → pop worker, discard from _connected_servers, RETURN
YES AND worker running → await worker.cleanup(); FINALLY: pop worker, discard from _connected_servers

activity_flows.name: _attempt_connect(server)
steps: SUCCESS → _connected_servers.add(server); if server was failed: _remove_failed_server(); pop from errors
CATCH asyncio.CancelledError → suppress or re-raise; _record_failure(server, exc, 'connect')
CATCH Exception → _record_failure(server, exc, 'connect'); if raise_on_error (strict): re-raise

activity_flows.name: _connect_all_parallel(servers)
steps: if not suppress_cancelled_error: raise first CancelledError found
YES → find first_failure (skip CancelledErrors if suppressing); raise its stored error

key_conditions_and_branches: failed_only: True → retry only failed_servers; False → full cleanup then retry all
connect_in_parallel: True → worker tasks; False → sequential loop
drop_failed_servers: True → exclude failures from active_servers; False → include all
strict: True → raise on first failure (sequential) or after gather (parallel)
suppress_cancelled_error: True → log+swallow CancelledError; False → propagate
worker.is_done: if worker task already finished, skip cleanup call

worker_pattern.purpose: Keeps connect/cleanup on same asyncio task (required by AnyIO cancel scopes)
worker_pattern.lifecycle: Created on first _get_worker(); destroyed after cleanup command

error_recovery_in_connect_all: If BaseException during sequential connect → _cleanup_servers([connected + failed]); restore previous active_servers; re-raise
If BaseException during parallel connect → _cleanup_servers(all servers_to_connect); restore previous active_servers; re-raise
important_notes: reconnect(failed_only=False) calls cleanup_all() first, ensuring proper teardown before retry
_servers_to_connect() skips already-connected servers — connect_all is idempotent
cleanup_all iterates reversed(_all_servers) — reverse order teardown
_cleanup_servers also reverses — consistent LIFO teardown semantics
active_servers is a property returning a copy of _active_servers list
Parallel mode uses dedicated _ServerWorker per server to preserve task affinity
